top5Opportunities.statement: Minimize the likelihood of emergency exit alarm silence during an extended power outage (>8 h) on an active Memory Care unit
Minimize the likelihood of a CMS Immediate Jeopardy citation resulting from an undocumented emergency exit elopement event
Minimize the time to detect a non-functional emergency exit guard during active Memory Care hours (target <4 h)
Minimize the likelihood of incomplete emergency exit inspection and event documentation at annual CMS survey
Minimize the likelihood of unmonitored Memory Care emergency exits being identified during a CMS site survey

needs.statement: Minimize the likelihood of emergency exit alarm silence during an extended power outage (>8 h) on an active Memory Care unit
needs.importanceRationale: Life-safety critical: Memory Care residents with dementia are elopement risks; a silent exit alarm during a power outage directly endangers residents and triggers CMS Immediate Jeopardy findings.
needs.satisfactionRationale: EM locks fail open during power outages, losing delay and monitoring. RFID wander systems lose monitoring without UPS. Only basic mechanical alarms remain power-independent, but lack remote notification.

needs.statement: Minimize the likelihood of a CMS Immediate Jeopardy citation resulting from an undocumented emergency exit elopement event
needs.importanceRationale: CMS Immediate Jeopardy citations can result in facility decertification and loss of Medicare/Medicaid reimbursement — existential financial risk for CCRC operators.
needs.satisfactionRationale: Real-time CMS-formatted documentation is absent in most deployments. Stanley Healthcare generates post-hoc logs but not automated real-time compliance packages.

needs.statement: Minimize the time to detect a non-functional emergency exit guard during active Memory Care hours (target <4 h)
needs.importanceRationale: A non-functional guard leaves an exit unprotected on a Memory Care unit; detection delays measured in hours create extended elopement windows.
needs.satisfactionRationale: No incumbent proactively detects mechanical failure — discovery is patrol-based only, with inspection intervals of several hours or longer.

needs.statement: Minimize the likelihood of incomplete emergency exit inspection and event documentation at annual CMS survey
needs.importanceRationale: Annual CMS surveys require comprehensive documentation of exit inspections and events; incomplete records trigger deficiency citations and corrective action plans.
needs.satisfactionRationale: Manual document preparation remains the norm across the industry. Stanley Healthcare provides partial coverage only in premium installations.

needs.statement: Minimize the likelihood of unmonitored Memory Care emergency exits being identified during a CMS site survey
needs.importanceRationale: CMS surveyors check every exit on Memory Care units; a single unmonitored door can trigger a plan-of-correction requirement and reputational damage.
needs.satisfactionRationale: EM lock and RFID deployments frequently leave secondary exits uncovered due to high retrofit cost per door. Coverage gaps are common, especially on older buildings.

needs.statement: Minimize the time between unauthorized emergency exit activation and staff notification during overnight and weekend staffing periods
needs.importanceRationale: Overnight and weekend shifts have minimal staffing; delayed notification of an exit breach significantly increases elopement risk for Memory Care residents.
needs.satisfactionRationale: Inovonics achieves reasonable overnight alerting in equipped facilities, but deployment is not universal. Many facilities rely on audible alarms that may not reach remote nursing stations.

needs.statement: Minimize the likelihood of undetected battery depletion creating a wireless monitoring gap on an emergency exit door
needs.importanceRationale: Battery-powered wireless devices are deployed on exits that lack mains wiring; undetected depletion silently removes monitoring capability.
needs.satisfactionRationale: Basic mechanical alarms have no battery monitoring at all. Inovonics supervised check-ins are the exception, not the norm in most CCRC deployments.

needs.statement: Minimize the time and effort required to generate emergency exit compliance documentation for CMS regulatory review
needs.importanceRationale: Staff hours spent compiling manual compliance documentation represent a significant operational burden, especially during survey preparation periods.
needs.satisfactionRationale: Manual preparation remains dominant across the industry. Documented automation for CMS-formatted exit compliance reports is rare.

needs.statement: Minimize the likelihood of the monitoring dashboard showing a guard as armed while the physical mechanism has been defeated or removed
needs.importanceRationale: A false-armed state creates a dangerous illusion of protection — staff believe the exit is secured when residents can freely exit.
needs.satisfactionRationale: Physical mechanism defeat is undetectable by electrical monitoring. Even bidirectional radio check-ins only detect radio silence, not hardware defeat or removal.

needs.statement: Minimize the time between emergency exit guard activation and Memory Care staff notification to less than 30 seconds
needs.importanceRationale: Rapid notification enables staff to intercept a resident before they leave the building; each additional second of delay increases elopement risk.
needs.satisfactionRationale: Inovonics and Stanley Healthcare achieve near-real-time mobile alerting in well-equipped facilities, adequately addressing this need in modern deployments.

needs.statement: Minimize the likelihood of IoT module battery depletion going undetected on a low-traffic CCRC wing, creating a wireless monitoring gap during overnight staffing periods
needs.importanceRationale: Low-traffic wings generate fewer door events, meaning battery consumption patterns are less predictable and gaps may persist unnoticed for days.
needs.satisfactionRationale: Most wireless door monitoring systems lack proactive battery health reporting. Supervised check-in protocols exist but are not standard in CCRC deployments.

needs.statement: Minimize the time to discover that a wireless receiver serving multiple door guardians on a single care level has lost connectivity, leaving all exits without event transmission while mechanical alarms remain functional
needs.importanceRationale: A receiver failure silently disables event transmission for an entire care level, creating a multi-door monitoring blind spot while mechanical alarms mask the problem.
needs.satisfactionRationale: Receiver health monitoring is not a standard feature. Most systems only detect individual device failures, not gateway-level connectivity loss.

needs.statement: Minimize the likelihood of wireless alarm event transmission failure from emergency exit doors in reinforced concrete corridors or basement wings exceeding the 30 m EasyWave transmitter range
needs.importanceRationale: CCRC buildings often include reinforced concrete corridors and below-grade wings that attenuate wireless signals, creating RF-challenging installation environments.
needs.satisfactionRationale: Wireless repeaters and mesh networks can mitigate range limitations but add cost and complexity. The 30 m EasyWave range is adequate for most standard corridors.

needs.statement: Minimize the likelihood of unauthorized guard removal or mechanism defeat by a cognitively impaired resident manipulating the exposed surface-mounted guard housing on the door handle
needs.importanceRationale: Cognitively impaired residents may repetitively attempt to manipulate or remove surface-mounted devices on door handles through persistent physical interaction.
needs.satisfactionRationale: Surface-mounted guards provide adequate tamper resistance for most residents. Dedicated anti-tamper housings exist but add cost and are not universally deployed.

needs.statement: Minimize the time to integrate EasyWave-protocol door guardian event streams into an existing BACnet or KNX building management platform deployed across a continuing care campus
needs.importanceRationale: CCRC campuses with existing BACnet/KNX infrastructure expect new devices to integrate into their unified building management dashboard.
needs.satisfactionRationale: EasyWave-to-BACnet/KNX protocol bridges exist but require custom integration work. No plug-and-play solution is available for most BMS platforms.
